nouns.clothes.shoes: You wear shoes on your feet to walk and run.
They have a soft inside, a hard bottom, and laces or straps.
You put your shoes on before you go outside.
nouns.clothes.socks: You wear socks on your feet inside your shoes.
They are soft and stretchy and come in pairs.
You pull on socks before your shoes.
nouns.clothes.shirt: You wear a shirt on your top half.
It covers your chest and has holes for your arms.
You pull a shirt over your head to get dressed.
nouns.clothes.pants: You wear pants on your legs.
They have two leg holes and cover you to your ankles.
You pull pants up to get dressed.
nouns.clothes.coat: You wear a coat to stay warm outside.
It is thick and cozy with buttons or a zipper.
You put on a coat when it is cold.
nouns.clothes.hat: You wear a hat on your head.
It sits on top and can warm you or shade the sun.
You put on a hat when you go outside.
nouns.clothes.pajamas: You wear pajamas to sleep in.
They are soft and cozy — a top and bottoms.
You put on pajamas at bedtime.
nouns.clothes.diaper: A diaper keeps a baby clean and dry.
It is soft and goes around the bottom.
A grown-up changes a diaper to keep you fresh.
nouns.clothes.more.t_shirt: A T-shirt is a soft shirt with short sleeves.
It slips over your head and is comfy.
You wear a T-shirt on a warm day.
nouns.clothes.more.jeans: They are made of thick blue cloth called denim.
You wear jeans to play and go out.
nouns.clothes.more.sweater: A sweater is a warm, cozy top.
It is knit from soft, thick yarn.
You wear a sweater when it is chilly.
nouns.clothes.more.jacket: A jacket is a light coat for cool days.
It has sleeves and a zipper or buttons.
You put on a jacket when it is a little cold.
nouns.clothes.more.scarf: A scarf wraps around your neck to keep it warm.
It is a long, soft strip of cloth.
You wrap a scarf on a cold day.
nouns.clothes.more.mittens: They cover your whole hand, with a spot for your thumb.
You wear mittens in the cold snow.
nouns.clothes.more.beanie: A beanie is a soft hat that keeps your head warm.
It is snug and stretchy and pulls over your ears.
You wear a beanie when it is cold.
nouns.clothes.more.baseball_cap: A baseball cap is a hat with a brim in front.
The brim shades your eyes from the sun.
You wear a cap on a sunny day.
nouns.clothes.more.sneakers: Sneakers are comfy shoes for running and playing.
They are soft and bouncy with laces.
You wear sneakers to run and jump.
nouns.clothes.more.rain_boots: Rain boots keep your feet dry in puddles.
They are tall and made of shiny rubber.
You wear rain boots to splash in the rain.
nouns.clothes.extra.dress: A dress covers your top and goes down like a skirt.
It slips over your head, often twirly at the bottom.
You might wear a dress on a special day.
nouns.clothes.extra.skirt: A skirt is clothing that hangs from your waist.
It covers your legs and can twirl when you spin.
You wear a skirt with a shirt on top.
nouns.clothes.extra.shorts: Shorts are short pants for warm days.
They cover your legs only down to your knees.
You wear shorts when it is hot outside.
nouns.clothes.extra.swimsuit: A swimsuit is what you wear in the water.
It is snug and dries fast.
You put on a swimsuit to swim.
nouns.clothes.extra.hoodie: A hoodie is a cozy sweater with a hood.
The hood pulls up over your head, and it has a big pocket.
You wear a hoodie to stay warm and comfy.
nouns.clothes.extra.slippers: Slippers are soft shoes you wear inside.
They are squishy and warm for your feet.
You wear slippers around the house.
nouns.clothes.extra.boots: Boots are tall shoes that cover your ankles.
They are sturdy and keep your feet warm and dry.
You wear boots in cold or muddy weather.
nouns.clothes.extra.sandals: Sandals are open shoes for warm days.
They have straps and let your toes peek out.
You wear sandals in the summer.
nouns.clothes.extra.gloves: Gloves keep your hands warm, with a spot for each finger.
They cover your whole hand and each finger separately.
You wear gloves in the cold.
nouns.clothes.extra.robe: A robe is a soft, cozy wrap you put on.
It ties at the waist and is warm and fluffy.
You wear a robe after a bath.
nouns.vehicles.bus: A bus carries lots of people at once.
It is long with many seats and big windows.
You ride a bus to school or around town.
nouns.vehicles.truck: A truck carries big, heavy loads.
It has a strong body and big wheels.
Trucks haul things along the road.
nouns.vehicles.train: A train carries people and things on tracks.
It is a long line of cars that goes 'choo-choo.'
Trains zoom along the railroad tracks.
nouns.vehicles.airplane: An airplane flies high in the sky.
It has big wings and zooms above the clouds.
You ride an airplane to travel far away.
nouns.vehicles.boat: A boat floats and travels on the water.
It glides across the top of the water.
You ride a boat on a lake or the sea.
nouns.vehicles.bike: A bike has two wheels, and you pedal it.
It has handlebars to steer and pedals to push.
You ride a bike outside, wearing a helmet.
nouns.vehicles.emerg.fire_truck: A fire truck races to put out fires.
It is big and red with a loud siren and a ladder.
Firefighters ride the fire truck to a fire.
nouns.vehicles.emerg.police_car: A police car helps keep people safe.
It has flashing lights and a siren.
A police officer drives the police car.
nouns.vehicles.emerg.ambulance: An ambulance rushes sick or hurt people to help.
It is white with flashing lights and a loud siren.
nouns.vehicles.emerg.helicopter: A helicopter flies with spinning blades on top.
Its blades whirl around to lift it up.
A helicopter can hover in one spot in the sky.
nouns.vehicles.work.tractor: A tractor is a strong machine for the farm.
It has huge back wheels and pulls heavy loads.
Farmers drive a tractor in the field.
nouns.vehicles.work.dump_truck: A dump truck carries dirt and rocks and dumps them out.
Its back tips up to pour the load.
Dump trucks work at building sites.
nouns.vehicles.work.garbage_truck: A garbage truck picks up the trash.
It has a big back that swallows the garbage.
The garbage truck comes to collect the trash.
nouns.vehicles.work.crane: A crane lifts heavy things up high.
It has a very tall arm with a hook.
nouns.vehicles.work.cement_mixer: A cement mixer spins wet cement to keep it ready.
It has a big spinning drum on the back.
nouns.vehicles.work.bulldozer: A bulldozer pushes dirt with a big flat blade.
It has a wide blade in front and crawler tracks.
Bulldozers clear the ground at building sites.
nouns.vehicles.personal.motorcycle: A motorcycle is like a fast bike with an engine.
It has two wheels and goes 'vroom.'
A grown-up rides a motorcycle with a helmet.
nouns.vehicles.personal.scooter: A scooter has wheels, and you push with one foot.
It has a board to stand on and a handle to steer.
You ride a scooter on the sidewalk.
nouns.vehicles.personal.skateboard: A skateboard is a board with four wheels.
You stand on it and push with your foot to roll.
You ride a skateboard, wearing a helmet.
nouns.vehicles.personal.taxi: A taxi is a car that gives people a ride.
It is often yellow with a light on top.
You can ride in a taxi to get somewhere.
nouns.vehicles.personal.race_car: A race car is built to go super fast.
It is low and sleek with big tires.
Race cars zoom around a track.
nouns.vehicles.personal.rv: An RV is a big van you can live in while you travel.
It has beds and a little kitchen inside.
Families take an RV on long trips.
nouns.vehicles.airsea.rocket: A rocket blasts off into space.
It is tall and pointy with fire and smoke at the bottom.
A rocket flies astronauts up to space.
nouns.vehicles.airsea.submarine: It is shaped like a tube and dives deep down.
A submarine explores under the sea.
nouns.vehicles.airsea.sailboat: A sailboat moves with the wind.
It has a big cloth sail that catches the breeze.
You sail a sailboat across the water.
nouns.vehicles.airsea.ship: A ship is a big boat that sails the sea.
It is huge and can carry lots of people and things.
Ships travel across the big ocean.
nouns.vehicles.airsea.hot_air: A hot air balloon floats up in the sky.
It is a giant balloon with a basket hanging below.
People ride in the basket and float along.
nouns.toys.ball: You throw, kick, bounce, and roll a ball when you play.
It is round, so it rolls, and comes in many sizes.
You play with a ball outside or in a big room.
nouns.toys.book: A book is full of stories and pictures.
It has pages you turn one at a time.
You read a book or have someone read it to you.
nouns.toys.blocks: Blocks are toys you stack to build things.
They are little squares and shapes you pile up.
You build towers with blocks, then knock them down.
nouns.toys.car: A toy car is a little car you push and zoom.
It has four tiny wheels that roll.
You race your toy car along the floor.
nouns.toys.bear: A teddy bear is a soft, cuddly toy.
It is fuzzy with button eyes and a sewn-on smile.
You hug your teddy bear at bedtime.
nouns.toys.doll: A doll is a toy that looks like a little person.
It has a face, hair, and tiny clothes.
You take care of your doll like a baby.
nouns.toys.puzzle: A puzzle is a picture cut into pieces you fit together.
Each piece has a special spot it fits into.
You solve a puzzle by matching the pieces.
nouns.toys.bubbles: Bubbles are floaty balls of soapy air.
They are round and shiny and pop when you touch them.
You blow bubbles and chase them outside.
nouns.toys.crayons: Crayons are sticks you use to color.
They come in lots of bright colors.
You color pictures with crayons on paper.
nouns.toys.train: A toy train is little cars that link together.
It rolls along a track and goes 'choo-choo.'
You push your toy train around the track.
nouns.toys.more.lego: Building bricks snap together to make things.
They are little colorful pieces that click together.
You build towers and cars with building bricks.
nouns.toys.more.stroller: A toy stroller pushes a doll around.
It has wheels and a seat for the doll.
You push your doll in the stroller as you play.
nouns.toys.more.dollhouse: A dollhouse is a tiny house for little dolls.
It has small rooms and tiny furniture inside.
You play pretend with the dolls in the dollhouse.
nouns.toys.more.kite: A kite flies up high on a string.
It is light, with a long tail, and catches the wind.
You fly a kite outside on a windy day.
nouns.toys.more.jumprope: A jump rope is a rope you swing and jump over.
You hold a handle in each hand and swing it.
You jump over the rope again and again.
nouns.toys.more.hula_hoop: A hula hoop is a big ring you spin around your waist.
It is a round hoop you wiggle to keep spinning.
You hula hoop by swinging your hips.
nouns.toys.more.play_doh: Play dough is soft, squishy stuff you shape.
It is colorful, and you can roll and squeeze it.
You make fun shapes with play dough.
nouns.toys.more.stickers: Stickers are little pictures that stick to things.
They peel off and have a sticky back.
You stick stickers on paper for fun.
nouns.toys.more.robot_toy: A toy robot is a machine you play with.
It can light up, move, and make beeps.
You play with a robot toy and pretend.
nouns.toys.more.action_figure: An action figure is a little hero toy.
It can bend its arms and legs to pose.
You play pretend adventures with it.
nouns.toys.more.marble: A marble is a tiny glass ball.
It is small, round, and swirly with color.
You roll marbles and watch them zoom.
nouns.toys.more.slinky: A slinky is a springy coil toy.
It is a long metal or plastic spring.
You can make a slinky walk down the stairs.
nouns.nature.sun: The sun is the bright star that lights up the day.
It is a giant glowing ball in the sky.
The sun warms us and shines in the daytime.
nouns.nature.moon: The moon glows in the night sky.
It is round and silvery and changes shape over the month.
You can see the moon at night.
nouns.nature.tree: A tree is a tall plant with a wooden trunk.
It has branches and leaves up high.
Trees give us shade, and birds nest in them.
nouns.nature.flower: A flower is the pretty, colorful part of a plant.
It has soft petals and a sweet smell.
Flowers bloom in the garden in spring.
nouns.nature.rain: Rain is water that falls from the clouds.
It comes down in little drops, drip-drop.
You wear boots and use an umbrella in the rain.
nouns.nature.snow: Snow is soft, white flakes that fall when it is cold.
Each tiny flake is cold and fluffy.
You build a snowman when it snows.
nouns.nature.star: A star is a tiny twinkling light in the night sky.
It looks like a sparkle far, far away.
You can see stars at night when it is dark.
nouns.nature.plants.cactus: A cactus is a prickly plant that lives in the desert.
It has sharp spines and stores water inside.
A cactus grows where it is dry and sunny.
nouns.nature.plants.fern: A fern is a leafy green plant.
It has feathery leaves that uncurl as they grow.
Ferns grow in shady, damp places.
nouns.nature.plants.daisy: A daisy is a cheerful flower.
It has white petals around a yellow middle.
Daisies grow in fields and gardens.
nouns.nature.plants.rose: A rose is a pretty flower that smells sweet.
It has soft petals and a thorny stem — careful.
Roses are given as a special gift.
nouns.nature.plants.sunflower: A sunflower is a tall, bright flower.
It has big yellow petals and turns toward the sun.
Sunflowers grow very tall in the summer.
nouns.nature.plants.tulip: A tulip is a colorful spring flower.
It is shaped like a little cup on a green stem.
Tulips bloom in many colors in the spring.
nouns.nature.plants.grass: Grass is the green plant that covers the ground.
It is made of thin green blades.
You run and play on the grass outside.
nouns.nature.plants.leaf: A leaf is the flat green part of a plant.
It grows on trees and turns colors in the fall.
Leaves flutter down from the trees.
nouns.nature.sky.cloud: A cloud is a fluffy white puff in the sky.
It is made of tiny water drops floating up high.
Clouds drift along, and rain comes from them.
nouns.nature.sky.planet: A planet is a giant round world in space.
It is a big ball that floats far out in space.
Earth is the planet we live on.
nouns.nature.sky.comet: A comet is a space rock with a glowing tail.
It zooms through space, trailing light behind it.
A comet is a special sight in the night sky.
nouns.nature.sky.astronaut: An astronaut is a person who goes to space.
They wear a big white suit and a round helmet.
Astronauts float and explore in space.
nouns.nature.sky.galaxy: A galaxy is a huge swirl of millions of stars.
It is a giant spiral of stars far out in space.
Our world is part of a galaxy.
nouns.sports.soccer: Soccer is a game where you kick a ball into a goal.
You run and kick a round ball with your feet.
You play soccer on a big grassy field.
nouns.sports.basketball: Basketball is a game where you toss a ball into a hoop.
You bounce and throw a big orange ball.
You play basketball on a court.
nouns.sports.baseball: Baseball is a game where you hit a ball with a bat.
You swing a bat and run around the bases.
You play baseball on a field with a team.
nouns.sports.football: Football is a game where you carry or throw a ball.
You toss a pointy brown ball and run for a touchdown.
You play football on a big field.
nouns.sports.tennis: Tennis is a game where you hit a ball over a net.
You swing a racket to bonk the ball back and forth.
You play tennis on a court.
nouns.sports.hockey: Hockey is a game where you hit a puck with a stick.
You slide on skates and shoot at a goal.
You play hockey on the ice.
nouns.sports.golf: Golf is a game where you hit a little ball into a hole.
You swing a long club to tap the ball.
You play golf on a big grassy course.
nouns.sports.bowling: Bowling is a game where you roll a ball to knock down pins.
You roll a heavy ball down a smooth lane.
You go bowling to knock over all the pins.
nouns.sports.skating: Skating is gliding along on skates.
You wear skates with wheels or blades and glide.
You can skate on ice or on the sidewalk.
nouns.sports.swimming: Swimming is moving through the water.
You kick your legs and paddle your arms.
You go swimming in a pool or a lake.
nouns.sports.gymnastics: Gymnastics is tumbling, flipping, and balancing.
You roll, jump, and balance with your whole body.
nouns.sports.dancing: Dancing is moving your body to the music.
You wiggle, spin, and step to the beat.
You can dance anywhere a fun song plays.
nouns.music.drum: A drum makes a 'boom' when you hit it.
It is round, and you tap the top with sticks or hands.
You bang a drum to make a beat.
nouns.music.guitar: A guitar makes music when you strum its strings.
It has a wooden body and six strings.
You strum a guitar to play a song.
nouns.music.piano: A piano makes music when you press the keys.
It has rows of black and white keys.
You press piano keys to play a tune.
nouns.music.violin: A violin makes music when you slide a bow over its strings.
It is small and wooden, and you tuck it under your chin.
You play a violin with a long bow.
nouns.music.trumpet: A trumpet makes a loud, bright sound when you blow it.
It is shiny gold with buttons to press.
You blow into a trumpet to play it.
nouns.music.flute: A flute makes a soft, high sound when you blow across it.
It is a long, thin silver tube with holes.
You hold a flute sideways and blow to play.
nouns.music.xylophone: A xylophone makes 'ding' sounds when you tap it.
It has colorful bars in a row, each a different note.
You hit the bars with little mallets.
nouns.music.tambourine: A tambourine jingles when you shake or tap it.
It is a round frame with little metal jingles.
You shake a tambourine to keep the beat.
nouns.music.maracas: Maracas make a 'shika-shika' sound when you shake them.
They are little handles with seeds rattling inside.
You shake maracas to the music.
nouns.music.harmonica: A harmonica makes music when you blow and breathe in.
It is a little box you hold up to your mouth.
You play a harmonica by blowing across the holes.
nouns.tools.hammer: A hammer pounds nails into wood.
It has a heavy metal head and a handle.
A grown-up uses a hammer to build things.
nouns.tools.screwdriver: A screwdriver turns screws to tighten things.
It has a handle and a tip that fits a screw.
A grown-up uses it to put things together.
nouns.tools.wrench: A wrench tightens and loosens bolts.
It is a metal tool that grips and turns.
A grown-up uses a wrench to fix things.
nouns.tools.saw: A saw cuts through wood — it is sharp, so careful.
It has a long blade with jagged teeth.
A grown-up uses a saw to cut wood.
nouns.tools.drill: A drill spins to make holes or drive screws.
It is a tool with a spinning bit and a trigger.
A grown-up uses a drill to build things.
nouns.tools.paintbrush: A paintbrush spreads paint on things.
It has soft bristles on the end of a handle.
You dip a paintbrush in paint to make art.
nouns.tools.toolbox: A toolbox holds and carries tools.
It is a sturdy box with a handle and a lid.
Tools are kept neat inside the toolbox.
